name: What evidence do I need for my on-job forklift injury claim?
description: Scene photos, medical records, witnesses, forklift inspection, OSHA reports.

mainEntity.name: What evidence do I need for my on-job forklift injury claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Scene photos, medical records, witnesses, forklift inspection, OSHA reports.
acceptedAnswer.description: Photos of scene/forklift, witness statements, medical records, OSHA logs, and incident report.[5] Context: Proves negligence like untrained operator or faulty equipment. Key factors: Surveillance video critical; expert analysis of forklift. Practical guidance: Don't let employer discard evidence; request preservation letter via attorney. Build strong file early.
